explanations.1: This tells Docker to use a specific syntax version for parsing the Dockerfile itself, enabling newer features.
explanations.24: The --mount flags create temporary cache folders during this RUN command so repeated apt-get installs reuse downloaded packages instead of downloading again.
explanations.48: Similar to line 24, this caches the yq download so if the image rebuilds, it won't re-download the same file.
explanations.64: This caches pip's package cache during the RUN command, so reinstalling Python packages on rebuild is faster.
explanations.78: This caches npm's package cache in the user's home directory, speeding up repeated npm installs.
explanations.114: The ${VAR:-default} syntax means 'use VAR if set, otherwise use the default value after the colon'.
explanations.127: From this point forward, all commands run as the vscode user instead of root, which is a security best practice.
explanations.130: This caches Quarto's tools directory so downloaded Chromium won't need to be re-downloaded on rebuild.
explanations.143: This long line defines a bash alias that finds all .dot files and converts them to .png images using the 'dot' command.
